title: Visa offices outside Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Visa offices outside Canada
Use this page to find the visa office that serves the country or territory you apply from. You may need to know this if
- your application has a visa office checklist For example, you need this checklist to sponsor a relative to immigrate to Canada. To apply, you must choose the visa office that serves your area. The documents required to apply may vary based on the visa office. Only some programs have visa office checklists.
For example, you need this checklist to sponsor a relative to immigrate to Canada.
- To apply, you must choose the visa office that serves your area.
- The documents required to apply may vary based on the visa office.
- Only some programs have visa office checklists.
- you fill out the web form and it asks for the visa office processing your application

## What visa offices do
Visa offices process applications for
- permanent residence (immigrating permanently)
- visitor visas (temporary resident visas)
- refugee resettlement
- permanent resident travel documents

## Wait for the visa office to contact you first
Don’t contact the visa office unless they reach out to you first.
- For example, the office sends you an email or letter to ask for a missing document for your application.
- In this case, the email or letter will tell you how to contact them.
You don’t need to contact a visa office to get the information you need. Find out how to
- give your biometrics
- check your application status
- check your application’s processing time
- ask about or update your application (web form)
- change your contact information (email or address)
- get answers in the Help Centre
